subject: Math
skill: Count, Read, and Write Numbers to 1,000

lesson.story_mission: Help the Number Trail Rangers repair missing signs on a trail that stretches all the way to 1,000.
lesson.mini_lesson: Numbers to 1,000 can be counted one at a time, read in word form, or skip-counted by equal jumps. Watch carefully at hundreds transitions: 198, 199, 200 and 998, 999, 1,000.
lesson.objective: Count, read, write, and skip-count numbers within 1,000.
lesson.concept_explanation: A sequence follows a rule. Counting by 1 changes to the next number, counting by 5s or 10s makes equal jumps, and counting by 100s changes the hundreds.
lesson.common_misconception: A child may skip over a hundred transition, such as saying 199, 201, because the new hundred sounds different.
lesson.real_world_connection: Counting and reading numbers helps with addresses, pages, game scores, and collecting large groups.
lesson.lesson_summary: Use place value and the counting rule to fill missing numbers and match numerals to word forms.

worked_examples.text: 342 is read as three hundred forty-two.

hint_ladder: Use hundreds, tens, and ones to check the number.
Say the full sequence aloud before choosing.

misconception_bank.sequence_gap_error: Missing or repeating a number when counting by ones.
misconception_bank.hundreds_transition_error: Losing the sequence when a new hundred or 1,000 appears.
misconception_bank.numeral_word_mismatch: Matching a numeral with a different word form.

story.text: Listen to this page. You are starting a math mission about Count, Read, and Write Numbers to 1,000. Look at the story and think about what the characters need to solve. Math missions are easier when you name the numbers and the model first. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson teaches Count, Read, and Write Numbers to 1,000. Look at the number sequence and connect each part to the math words. Use the vocabulary on the page to explain your thinking. Remember to check place value, units, or symbols before you answer. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This page lets you watch a worked example for Count, Read, and Write Numbers to 1,000. Follow the model one step at a time. Say what each number means before you look at the final answer. If a symbol appears, read it in words like plus, minus, equals, greater than, or less than. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo helps you try Count, Read, and Write Numbers to 1,000 with support. Build or study the model first, then connect it to the question. A good strategy is to explain the answer in math words before choosing. Press Next when you are ready for practice.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This is practice. Use the visual model to help you think about Count, Read, and Write Numbers to 1,000. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Check your strategy before you choose or write an answer. When you are ready, answer the question and keep going.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This is a challenge. Use the visual model to help you think about Count, Read, and Write Numbers to 1,000. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Check your strategy before you choose or write an answer. When you are ready, answer the question and keep going.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This is your checkpoint. Use the visual model to help you think about Count, Read, and Write Numbers to 1,000. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Check your strategy before you choose or write an answer. When you are ready, answer the question and keep going.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: This badge page celebrates your mission work on Count, Read, and Write Numbers to 1,000. Look at your stars and remember the strategy you used. Mistakes help show what to practice next. Save your growth data to continue.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page shows your growth for Count, Read, and Write Numbers to 1,000. Look at attempts, correct answers, accuracy, and hints used. Use the recommendation to decide what to practice next. You can continue to Skill Practice or replay the mission.
profile.label: Read This Page
